lede: A look at April 13 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Karl Hein (footballer) and Baron Davis, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Rory McIlroy
events.desc: Rory McIlroy wins the Masters Tournament, becoming just the sixth person to complete the Grand Slam in golf.

events.title: Kamień Pomorski homeless hostel fire
events.desc: A fire destroys a homeless hostel and kills at least 22 people in Kamień Pomorski, Poland.

events.title: Tiger Woods
events.desc: Tiger Woods becomes the youngest golfer to win the Masters Tournament.

events.title: Lapua Cartridge Factory explosion
events.desc: Forty workers die in the Lapua Cartridge Factory explosion, the deadliest industrial accident in modern Finnish history.

events.title: Oxygen
events.desc: At 10:08 PM EST an oxygen tank aboard the Apollo 13 Service Module explodes, putting the crew in great danger and causing major damage to the Apollo command and service module (codenamed "Odyssey") while en route to the Moon.

events.title: Central Intelligence Agency
events.desc: CIA director Allen Dulles launches the mind-control program Project MKUltra.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The discovery of mass graves of Polish prisoners of war killed by Soviet forces in the Katyń Forest Massacre is announced, causing a diplomatic rift between the Polish government-in-exile in London and the Soviet Union, which denies responsibility.

events.title: Raleigh, North Carolina
events.desc: American Civil War: Raleigh, North Carolina is occupied by Union forces.

events.title: Roman Catholic Relief Act 1829
events.desc: The Roman Catholic Relief Act 1829 gives Roman Catholics in the United Kingdom the right to vote and to sit in Parliament.

events.title: American Revolutionary War
events.desc: American Revolutionary War: American forces are ambushed and defeated in the Battle of Bound Brook, New Jersey.

births.name: Harold Stassen
births.line: American lawyer and politician, 25th Governor of Minnesota (died 2001)

deaths.text: Moya Brennan, Irish singer-songwriter and harp player
Muriel Spark, Scottish novelist, poet, and critic
Culbert Olson, American lawyer and politician, 29th Governor of California
Jean de La Fontaine, French author and poet
